description: EN 16931 invoice semantic root.
information about allowances applicable to the Invoice as a whole.
information about charges and taxes other than VAT, applicable to the Invoice as a whole. The amount of a charge, without
information about VAT breakdown by different categories, rates and exemption reasons Sum of all taxable amounts subject
information on individual Invoice lines.
information about allowances applicable to the individual Invoice line.
information about charges and taxes other than VAT applicable to the individual Invoice line.
A group of business terms providing information about properties of the goods and services invoiced.
Identifier of the supplier to whom payment is due or from whom unused credits have been applied.
The name of the supplier.
If the organization segments suppliers into groups, the group to which this supplier is assigned.
The physical street address line 1 of the supplier.
The physical street address line 2 of the supplier.
The physical city where the supplier is located.
The physical state or province where the supplier is located. Recommend ISO 3166-2.
The zip code of the city where the supplier is physically located.
The country code where the supplier is physically located. Recommend ISO 3166-1 Alpha 2 or ISO 3166-1 Alpha 3 format (XX or XXX).
The supplier’s tax identification number.
The billing address line 1 of the supplier.
The billing address line 2 of the supplier.
The billing city of the supplier.
The billing state or province of the supplier. Recommend ISO 3166-2.
The billing zip code of the supplier’s city.
The billing country code of the supplier. Recommend ISO 3166-1 Alpha 2 or ISO 3166-1 Alpha 3 format (XX or XXX).
Date the supplier declared active.
Date the supplier was declared inactive.
The per invoice credit limit established with this supplier.
The credit limit for the total outstanding balance approved for the supplier.

The discount percentage the supplier may provide if an invoice is paid before a certain number of days. In the flat file, terms are represented as digits to one decimal place (for example, 10% would be represented as 10.0). In extensible business reporting language global ledger taxonomy framework (XBRL GL), the three fields Terms_Discount_Percentage, Terms_Discount_Days and Terms_Due_Days would be entered in the form "xx.x% dd Net dd," such as 2% 10 Net 30 for 2% discount if paid within 10 days, with the net due in 30 days.
The number of days from the invoice date the supplier allows to take advantage of discounted terms. Terms are represented as digits with no decimal places (for example, nnn).
The number of days allowed to meet the obligation before an invoice becomes overdue.
User_ID (from User_Listing file) for person who created the record.

The date the transaction was entered into the system. This is sometimes referred to as the creation date. This should be a system- generated date (rather than user-entered date), when possible. This date does not necessarily correspond with the date of the transaction itself.
The time this transaction was entered into the system. ISO 8601 representing time in 24-hour time (hhmm) (for example, 1:00 PM = 1300).
User ID (from User_Listing file) for person who approved the entry.
The date the entry was approved.
The time the entry was approved. ISO 8601 representing time in 24-hour time (hhmm) (for example, 1:00 PM = 1300).
User_ID (from User_Listing file) for the last person modifying this entry.
The date the entry was last modified.
The time the entry was last modified. ISO 8601 representing time in 24-hour time (hhmm) (for example, 1:00 PM = 1300).
Name of the primary contact at the supplier.
Phone number of the primary contact at the supplier.
Email address of the primary contact at the supplier.
